chain_name: Design System Harmonization — Luminous Pastels + WCAG AA
orchestrator_notes: Agents: Read this file BEFORE starting. If orchestrator_directives contains STOP, halt immediately. All design decisions are finalized in the spec document. Reference mockups at frontend/mock/ (gitignored but present locally). The design-system-sample-v2.html is the authoritative visual reference. Key changes: agent colors → Luminous Pastels, --text-muted → #8895a8, --text-secondary → #a3aac4, tinted badges (rgba 0.15 bg + bright text), square badge geometry (border-radius 8px), stat pills replace donuts. Status colors are UNCHANGED.

Update --border-subtle from rgba(255,255,255,0.08) to rgba(255,255,255,0.10)
Update agent-colors.scss CSS custom properties to Luminous Pastels
Add --badge-text: #0e1c2d token
Add tinted background rgba variants for each agent
Update variables.scss if agent colors referenced there
Audit all design-token-exempt comments for validity
Run npm run lint to verify zero errors

Updated --color-text-secondary from #8f97b7 to #a3aac4 in main.scss

variables.scss: no agent color references found — no changes needed
global-tabs.scss: no agent color or affected token references — no changes needed

Test files (agentColors.claude-code.spec.js, useAgentData.spec.js, AgentJobModal.spec.js) will also need updates when JS values change. The SCSS foundation is complete — all CSS custom properties and SCSS variables now use Luminous Pastels. variables.scss $lightest-blue (#8f97b7) is a general palette color used for scrollbar hover, not an agent color — leave it.

Components using SCSS $color-agent-* variables will get new colors on rebuild
New --agent-*-tinted vars available for badge backgrounds in 0870d+

sessions.title: Core Badge Components
planned_tasks: Update RoleBadge.vue — tinted style, square geometry (border-radius 8px)
Update StatusBadge.vue — tinted chip approach where applicable
Verify StatusChip.vue — status colors unchanged
Verify ActionIcons.vue — icon colors
Update GiljoFaceIcon.vue — verify fill colors
Verify GilMascot.vue — no conflicts
Run existing component tests
tasks_completed: RoleBadge.vue: replaced Vuetify v-chip with tinted span — rgba(color, 0.15) background + bright text, border-radius 8px. Role colors: owner=#AC80CC, admin=#6DB3E4, member=#5EC48E, viewer=#8895a8. Supports x-small/small/default sizes.

StatusBadge.vue: replaced flat v-chip with tinted span — rgba(statusColor, 0.15) background + bright status color text, border-radius 8px. Status colors unchanged: active=#67bd6d, inactive=#9e9e9e, completed=#67bd6d, cancelled=#ff9800, terminated=#e07872, deleted=#e07872.
StatusChip.vue: verified — uses getStatusConfig() from statusConfig.js, all status colors unchanged (waiting/working/blocked/complete/silent/decommissioned/handed_over). No changes needed.
ActionIcons.vue: verified — uses getActionConfig() from actionConfig.js for button colors, no agent colors or hardcoded hex values. No changes needed.
GiljoFaceIcon.vue: verified — uses SVG file references (/icons/Giljo_YW_Face.svg, /icons/Giljo_Inactive_Dark.svg), no hardcoded colors. No changes needed.
GilMascot.vue: verified — body fill uses rgb(var(--v-theme-primary)), eyes use currentColor/theme vars. No agent color conflicts. No changes needed.
Updated StatusBadge.spec.js test assertions to match new DOM structure (span.status-badge instead of v-chip, tinted style checks, updated computed property names).
npm run lint: 0 errors, 7 pre-existing warnings (unchanged). npm run test: 96 files, 1893 tests pass, 0 failures.
deviations: RoleBadge and StatusBadge were not imported by any other component — changes are self-contained with no downstream breakage risk.

StatusBadge previously used Vuetify semantic color names (success, warning, error) and hex (#fff) via color prop. Replaced with explicit hex values matching the existing semantic colors for the tinted pattern.
sessions.notes_for_next: 0870e: Both RoleBadge and StatusBadge now use plain HTML spans with inline tinted styles instead of Vuetify v-chip. Components consuming these in the future should not rely on v-chip classes. The hexToRgb helper is duplicated in both components — if more tinted components emerge, consider extracting to a shared utility. StatusChip.vue (job statuses) still uses Vuetify v-chip with statusConfig colors — this is intentional as job status colors are unchanged and the chip includes tooltip/health indicator features that benefit from Vuetify integration.
cascading_impacts: RoleBadge no longer renders as v-chip — any future consumer expecting .v-chip class will need adjustment
StatusBadge no longer renders as v-chip — same consideration applies
StatusBadge status colors are now explicit hex values rather than Vuetify theme names — decoupled from Vuetify theme changes
sessions.summary: Converted RoleBadge.vue and StatusBadge.vue to tinted style (rgba 0.15 background + bright text) with square geometry (border-radius 8px). Verified 4 other components unchanged: StatusChip.vue, ActionIcons.vue, GiljoFaceIcon.vue, GilMascot.vue. Updated StatusBadge test file. 3 files changed, all 1893 tests pass.

sessions.title: Navigation & Layout Shell

Verify AppBar.vue — ActiveProductDisplay chip, ConnectionStatus colors
Verify DefaultLayout.vue — no hardcoded colors
Verify ActiveProductDisplay.vue chip styling
Verify ConnectionStatus.vue chip styling
Verify NotificationDropdown.vue badge colors
Run existing tests
tasks_completed: Giljo_Inactive_Dark.svg: updated fill from #A5AAAF to #8f97b7 (matching MDI --nav-icon-color)
NavigationDrawer.vue: added opacity 0.85 on inactive Jobs icon, full opacity on active state. Active SVG already correct (#ffc300 body, #e1e1e1 eyes).
AppBar.vue: verified — uses Vuetify semantic colors (error, primary, success, grey) for role chips, $color-brand-yellow for about link. No hardcoded old agent colors.
DefaultLayout.vue: verified — no hardcoded colors at all, pure layout component.
ActiveProductDisplay.vue: verified — uses Vuetify semantic colors (primary, grey) only. No hardcoded old colors.
ConnectionStatus.vue: verified — uses Vuetify semantic colors (success, warning, error, grey, info) throughout. No hardcoded old colors.
NotificationDropdown.vue: verified — uses Vuetify semantic colors and theme CSS variables throughout. No hardcoded old agent colors.
npm run lint: 0 errors, 7 pre-existing warnings (unchanged). npm run test: 96 files, 1893 tests pass, 0 failures.
deviations: SVG is loaded via v-img (renders as tag), so CSS custom properties cannot penetrate the SVG. The fill color was updated directly in the SVG file rather than via a CSS variable. The opacity is controlled via inline style binding on the v-img element.
sessions.notes_for_next: 0870f: All navigation and layout shell components are clean. No hardcoded old colors remain in the app shell. The Jobs icon now uses #8f97b7 inactive fill (matching MDI icons) with 0.85 opacity, and #ffc300/#e1e1e1 active state. AppBar role chips use Vuetify semantic color names (not hex), so they'll follow the Vuetify theme — no action needed. ConnectionStatus and NotificationDropdown also use Vuetify semantics exclusively.
cascading_impacts: Giljo_Inactive_Dark.svg fill color change affects any component that references this SVG — currently only NavigationDrawer.vue uses it
The opacity 0.85 on inactive state is applied via dynamic style binding, no CSS class changes needed
sessions.summary: Fixed Jobs nav icon: SVG fill #A5AAAF → #8f97b7, opacity 0.85 inactive / 1.0 active. Verified 5 other layout components (AppBar, DefaultLayout, ActiveProductDisplay, ConnectionStatus, NotificationDropdown) — all clean, using Vuetify semantic colors only. 2 files changed, all 1893 tests pass.

sessions.title: Dashboard View Redesign
planned_tasks: Restructure DashboardView.vue — stat pills row, mini stats, projects panel, bottom grid (360 memories + git commits)
Replace DonutChart.vue usage with inline stat pill + micro-bar pattern
Update RecentProjectsList.vue — new table row styling
Update RecentMemoriesList.vue — tinted type tags
Reference mockup: frontend/mock/dashboard-enhanced.html
tasks_completed: DashboardView.vue: complete restructure — removed donut chart section, replaced with 3-column stat pills row (status distribution, taxonomy, agent roles) each with big number + stacked micro-bar + micro-legend. Added 6-column mini stats row (active, tasks, API calls, MCP calls, exec auto, exec supervised). Projects panel is now full-width with panel-header pattern. Bottom 2-column grid: 360 memories (left) with brain icon + tinted type tags, git commits (right) with SHA + message. Added header with product label and clock. Responsive breakpoints at 1100px, 960px, 600px.

RecentProjectsList.vue: replaced v-list with custom project-row divs — taxonomy shown as tinted badge (rgba 0.15 bg + bright text), project name with ellipsis, mono timestamp, pill-shaped status chips with semantic colors (active=white, completed=green, cancelled=yellow, terminated=pink). Hover state with negative margin expansion.

RecentMemoriesList.vue: replaced v-list with custom memory-row divs — brain icon in yellow-tinted square, 2-line clamped summary text, mono meta line (project · product · relative time), tinted type tags mapped to agent colors (closeout=implementer blue, completion=documenter green, handover=reviewer purple, archived=analyzer coral). Dialog retained with updated tinted type badge.
DonutChart.vue import removed from DashboardView — component still exists but is no longer used anywhere. Can be safely deleted in a cleanup pass.
Accessible text colors used throughout: --text-muted #8895a8 for labels/timestamps, --text-secondary #a3aac4 for legends/subtitles.
npm run lint: 0 errors, 7 pre-existing warnings (unchanged). npm run test: 96 files, 1893 tests pass, 0 failures.
deviations: DonutChart.vue was not deleted — it was only removed from import. The file remains in case future cleanup wants a formal deprecation or in case another view references it. Grep confirms no other .vue file imports it.

The mockup's 'projects' panel shows project IDs (FE-0020, BE-0025) which don't exist in the current data model. RecentProjectsList uses taxonomy_alias as the leading badge instead, which maps to the existing API response structure.

Mini stats 'Exec: Auto' aggregates all autonomous execution modes (multi_terminal + claude_code_cli + codex_cli + gemini_cli) rather than showing them individually, since the mockup shows only 6 slots and the old dashboard had 4 separate execution mode cards.
sessions.notes_for_next: 0870g: DonutChart.vue is now orphaned — no component imports it. It can be deleted when convenient or left for a cleanup session. The chart.js dependency (vue-chartjs, chart.js) may also become removable if no other component uses it — check before removing. ProductSelector.vue was not modified (verified clean, uses Vuetify semantic colors). The dashboard now uses CSS custom properties var(--text-muted) and var(--text-secondary) extensively — these were set up in 0870a. The stat pill pattern is pure CSS with inline styles for segment colors, no chart library needed.
cascading_impacts: DonutChart.vue is no longer imported — chart.js dependency may be removable if unused elsewhere
RecentProjectsList.vue no longer uses v-list or v-chip — consumers expecting Vuetify list-item classes will need adjustment
RecentMemoriesList.vue no longer uses v-list for the main list — dialog still uses Vuetify v-dialog/v-card
Dashboard layout is now fully custom CSS grid — no longer depends on Vuetify v-row/v-col for the main content area (banners still use v-container)
sessions.summary: Replaced donut charts with stat pill + micro-bar pattern. New layout: 3 stat pills → 6 mini stat counters → full-width projects panel → 2-column bottom grid (360 memories + git commits). Updated RecentProjectsList with tinted taxonomy badges and status pill chips. Updated RecentMemoriesList with brain icon, tinted type tags mapped to agent colors, and compact row layout. 3 files changed, all 1893 tests pass.

sessions.title: Welcome/Home View Redesign
planned_tasks: Rewrite WelcomeView.vue — hero (giljo face + glow + greeting + tagline), quick-launch cards (dynamic), Your Team grid, conditional section (setup on first login, recent projects otherwise)
Verify SetupWizardOverlay.vue compatibility
Reference mockup: frontend/mock/welcome-v3-colors.html
tasks_completed: WelcomeView.vue: complete rewrite — hero section with GilMascot wrapped in CSS drop-shadow glow (yellow + green), float animation (4s ease-in-out), radial gradient glow pulse behind mascot. Personalized greeting logic preserved verbatim (time-based pools: withComma, withoutComma, funCasual).
Hero subtitle: website tagline 'The context engineering platform for AI-assisted development. Define your product once — every agent starts with the full picture.' with dimmed second line.

Quick-launch cards: 3 dynamic cards adapting to product state. No active product: New Product, Dashboard, Task Board. Active product: New Project (with product name), Active Projects (with count badge), Task Board. Each card has tinted icon square, title, description, optional mono badge. Hover: translateY(-3px) + accent top-border reveal.

Your Team section: fetches templates from api.templates.list(), maps to tinted square badges (rgba 0.15 bg + bright agent color text, border-radius 12px) using getAgentColor(). Shows active count / total slots pill, empty slots with dashed border + plus icon. Manage link routes to /Settings.

Conditional bottom section: if setup not complete, shows Getting Started CTA card (rocket icon, title, description, chevron) that triggers SetupWizardOverlay. If setup complete and projects exist, shows Recent Projects panel reusing RecentProjectsList component with panel header + 'All Projects →' link.
Footer: 'Community Edition · v{version}' with dot separator, version fetched from api.stats.getSystem().

SetupWizardOverlay integration: all props, events, and wizard state logic preserved exactly (showSetupOverlay, setupStep, setupSelectedTools, handleStepComplete with step 0-3 handlers, handleDismiss, auto-launch on first login or openSetup query param).

Atmosphere gradient background (fixed position, yellow + blue radial gradients). Staggered fadeSlideUp animations on all sections. Responsive breakpoint at 960px (single-column quick cards, smaller team cards).
npm run lint: 0 errors, 7 pre-existing warnings (unchanged). npm run test: 96 files, 1893 tests pass, 0 failures.
deviations: Mockup uses inline SVG for the hero mascot; implementation wraps the existing GilMascot.vue component (which has blinking eyes animation) inside a filter/glow wrapper div, achieving the same visual effect while preserving the blink behavior.
Mockup has a Solid/Tinted badge style toggle for the team section — omitted in implementation since the 0870 design system standardizes on tinted badges only (no toggle needed).
Version is fetched from api.stats.getSystem() at runtime; if the API call fails, the version field is silently empty rather than showing a hardcoded value.
Template total slots defaults to 8; attempts to read max_slots from api.templates.activeCount() response but gracefully falls back if not present in the API response.
sessions.notes_for_next: 0870h: WelcomeView.vue is fully rewritten. It now imports RecentProjectsList from dashboard/, useProjectStore, and api.templates. The setup wizard integration is unchanged — SetupWizardOverlay.vue was not modified, only verified compatible (all props and events match). The quick-launch cards use hardcoded agent color hex values (#6DB3E4, #AC80CC) for icon tinting rather than CSS custom properties, since these are inline styles on dynamic cards. DonutChart.vue remains orphaned from 0870f — still safe to delete. The GilMascot glow effect is pure CSS (filter: drop-shadow + radial-gradient background), no new dependencies added.
cascading_impacts: WelcomeView now imports RecentProjectsList, useProjectStore, and api (templates + stats) — more API calls on mount than before
The old welcome layout (v-container + v-card + v-row quick nav buttons) is completely replaced — any test asserting on old DOM structure will need updating
SetupWizardOverlay integration is unchanged — no downstream impact on setup flow
sessions.summary: Complete rewrite of WelcomeView.vue matching v3 mockup. Hero: GilMascot with CSS glow + float animation + personalized greeting + website tagline. Quick-launch: 3 dynamic cards adapting to product state. Your Team: active templates from API with tinted square badges + empty slots. Conditional bottom: setup CTA or recent projects. Footer: Community Edition + version. 1 file changed (658 insertions, 91 deletions), all 1893 tests pass.

sessions.title: Projects & Tasks Views
planned_tasks: Restyle ProjectsView.vue — tinted status chips, square project ID badges, accessible text
Restyle TasksView.vue — tinted status/priority pills, search/filter bar
Reference mockup: frontend/mock/projects-tasks-view.html
tasks_completed: ProjectsView.vue: replaced Vuetify v-chip taxonomy badges with tinted square spans — rgba(color, 0.15) background + bright text, border-radius 4px. Dynamic projectIdBadgeStyle() helper generates hex-based rgba backgrounds via 26 suffix (~15% opacity).
ProjectsView.vue: project UUID text updated to use --text-muted (#8895a8) via .project-uuid-text class with IBM Plex Mono font, 0.58rem size.
ProjectsView.vue: staged column converted from Vuetify v-chip to tinted span chips — Yes: green tinted ($color-status-success at 12% opacity), No: coral tinted ($color-agent-analyzer at 12% opacity).
ProjectsView.vue: date cells updated with .date-cell class — 0.72rem font, --text-secondary color, empty dates use --text-muted.

ProjectsView.vue: table panel gets smooth-border (inset box-shadow 1px $color-border-subtle), 16px border-radius. Table headers: uppercase 0.6rem with muted color, 0.06em letter-spacing. Row hover: rgba(255,255,255,0.02). Cell separators: rgba(255,255,255,0.04).

TasksView.vue: status chips converted from Vuetify v-chip to tinted spans — pending=yellow (brand-yellow 12%), in_progress=blue (implementor 12%), completed=green (status-success 15%), cancelled=pink (#c6298c 12%). Pill-shaped with 9999px border-radius.

TasksView.vue: priority chips converted from Vuetify v-chip to tinted pills — critical/high: analyzer coral tinted, medium: subtle 5% white bg with secondary text, low: 3% white bg with muted text. Square with 4px border-radius.
TasksView.vue: filter bar restyled from v-row/v-col grid to flex layout — search input with smooth-border focus ring (brand-yellow 30%), selects with smooth-border, clear button in muted text.
TasksView.vue: task title text colored with brand-yellow ($color-brand-yellow), description uses muted text ($color-text-muted) with 2-line clamp.
TasksView.vue: category column uses .category-pill (pill-shaped, 5% white bg, secondary text). Convert column uses styled .row-action button with yellow color and opacity hover effect.
TasksView.vue: table panel smooth-border (16px border-radius), table header with Outfit font. Same row hover/separator pattern as ProjectsView.
TasksView.vue: removed unused getStatusTextStyle() and getPriorityColor() functions (replaced by CSS classes).
npm run lint: 0 errors, 7 pre-existing warnings (unchanged). npm run test: 96 files, 1893 tests pass, 0 failures.
deviations: ProjectsView taxonomy badge uses hex + '26' suffix for 15% opacity (e.g. #E0787226) rather than CSS rgba(), because the color comes from a JS variable (project_type.color) and parsing hex to rgb channels for inline rgba() would be unnecessarily complex. The visual result is identical.
TasksView status chips no longer use Vuetify v-chip — replaced with plain HTML spans and CSS classes. The inline v-select dropdown for status changes still works, only the selected display changed.
TasksView priority dropdown items now show the tinted pill in the prepend slot instead of a Vuetify v-chip color swatch.
sessions.notes_for_next: 0870i: Both ProjectsView and TasksView now use the tinted chip pattern with accessible text colors. StatusBadge (updated in 0870d) is still used for the project status column — it already uses the tinted style. The v-data-table component itself is preserved (Vuetify pagination, sorting, search all still work). The scoped styles override Vuetify defaults via :deep() selectors for table headers, row hover, and cell separators. Both files use SCSS @use imports for design-tokens and variables. The filter bar in TasksView uses v-text-field/v-select with variant='solo' and flat to allow custom box-shadow styling via :deep().
cascading_impacts: ProjectsView taxonomy badge no longer renders as v-chip — any test asserting .v-chip class on taxonomy display will need adjustment
ProjectsView staged column no longer renders as v-chip — same consideration
TasksView status display no longer renders as v-chip — inline v-select still works but the selected value renders as a plain span
TasksView priority display no longer renders as v-chip — same pattern as status
Both views add :deep() overrides on .v-data-table elements — these may cascade if other styles target the same selectors
sessions.summary: Restyled ProjectsView.vue and TasksView.vue to match 0870 design system. ProjectsView: tinted square taxonomy badges (border-radius 4px), muted UUID text, tinted staged chips, accessible date colors, smooth-border panel. TasksView: tinted status chips (4 statuses), tinted priority pills, restyled filter bar, brand-yellow task titles, muted descriptions, category pills, styled actions. Removed 2 unused functions. 2 files changed (435 insertions, 186 deletions), all 1893 tests pass.

planned_tasks: Update ProductsView.vue, ProductDetailView.vue, ProductForm.vue
Update all product dialogs: ProductDetailsDialog, ProductDeleteDialog, ActivationWarningDialog, DeletedProductsRecoveryDialog
Update ProductTuningMenu.vue, ProductTuningReview.vue
tasks_completed: ProductsView.vue: Active status badge converted from v-chip to tinted span (rgba(103,189,109,0.15) bg + #67bd6d text, pill-shaped). Product cards use smooth-border class (border-radius 12px). Vision doc chips converted from v-chip to tinted spans with rgba backgrounds. Stat numbers use --text-secondary (#a3aac4). Labels/timestamps use --text-muted (#8895a8). Removed old product-card box-shadow white border.

ProductDetailView.vue: card wrapped with smooth-border class (border-radius 12px). Field labels updated from text-medium-emphasis to explicit #8895a8 color. Product ID uses monospace font at 0.75rem with #a3aac4 color.

ProductDetailsDialog.vue: Product ID uses IBM Plex Mono font, #8895a8 color. Summary level chips (Light/Medium/Full) converted from Vuetify v-chip to tinted spans — Light: green rgba, Medium: yellow rgba, Full: blue rgba. Consolidated vision chips also converted. Stat labels use #8895a8. All text-medium-emphasis replaced with detail-text-muted class (#8895a8). Added scoped style for summary-level-chip class.
ProductForm.vue: bordered-tabs-content border replaced with smooth-border pattern (inset box-shadow rgba(255,255,255,0.10)). All text-medium-emphasis replaced with form-text-muted class (#8895a8).

npm run lint: 0 errors, 7 pre-existing warnings (unchanged). npm run test: 96 files, 1893 tests pass, 0 failures.
deviations: ProductDeleteDialog.vue required no changes — it uses BaseDialog and has no text-medium-emphasis or hardcoded old colors. BaseDialog styling will be updated in 0870m.
ProductDetailsDialog summary chips now use v-progress-circular for loading state instead of the v-chip :loading prop, since plain spans don't have a built-in loading state.
ProductTuningReview's getConfidenceColor() was renamed to getConfidenceStyle() since it now returns inline style strings instead of Vuetify theme color names.
sessions.notes_for_next: 0870j: All product views and dialogs now use the 0870 design system. ProductDeleteDialog and ActivationWarningDialog use BaseDialog — when BaseDialog is updated in 0870m, these will automatically inherit the new styling. The summary-level-chip pattern (tinted span with rgba bg) is now used in ProductDetailsDialog — same pattern as the dashboard and projects views. ProductTuningReview: tinted drift/confidence chips, smooth-border value cards. ProductDeleteDialog: no changes needed. 8 files changed (218 insertions, 124 deletions), all 1893 tests pass.

sessions.title: Jobs/Orchestration Views
planned_tasks: Restyle LaunchTab.vue — execution mode selector, three-panel, tinted agent badges (square)
Restyle JobsTab.vue — table rows, tinted badges, phase badges, status text, msg badges, composer
Update AgentTableView.vue — same treatment
Update ProjectLaunchView.vue header styling
Reference mockup: frontend/mock/orchestration-view.html
tasks_completed: LaunchTab.vue: three panels restyled with bg-raised background, smooth-border (inset box-shadow rgba 0.10), border-radius 16px. Panel headers: 0.72rem uppercase with letter-spacing 0.06em, secondary color, border-bottom separator. Panel content: padding 14px 16px, description at 0.8rem, mission text italic/secondary color.

LaunchTab.vue: agent cards converted from pill-shaped with colored borders to flat rows with square tinted badges — rgba(color, 0.15) background + bright agent color text, border-radius 8px, 36px size. Status text uses SCSS design tokens ($color-status-*). Agent meta shows monospace ID. Edit/info icons use muted color with secondary hover.
LaunchTab.vue: removed orchestrator-card class (colored borders), inline cursor style. Added hexToRgba() and getAgentBadgeStyle() helpers. Responsive breakpoint at 1100px for single-column panels.

JobsTab.vue: table container converted to smooth-border panel (bg-raised, border-radius 16px, inset box-shadow). Table headers: 0.6rem uppercase, muted color, letter-spacing 0.06em. Row hover: rgba(255,255,255,0.02). Cell separators: rgba(255,255,255,0.04).

JobsTab.vue: agent avatar replaced from round v-avatar to square tinted badge (32px, border-radius 8px, rgba 0.15 bg + bright text). Agent name 0.8rem, skills label 0.62rem muted. Added hexToRgba() and getAgentBadgeStyle() helpers.

JobsTab.vue: phase badge converted from solid amber to tinted (rgba(251,192,45,0.15) bg + #FBC02D text). Play/copy button converted from inset-border ring to tinted yellow bg (rgba 0.12) with hover 0.2. Faded state: transparent bg, muted color, opacity 0.2.

JobsTab.vue: messages waiting badge converted to tinted circular badges — zero: rgba(103,189,109,0.12) bg + #67bd6d text, has-msgs: rgba(255,152,0,0.15) bg + #ff9800 text. Duration/steps cells use IBM Plex Mono, 0.72rem, secondary color.
JobsTab.vue: action icons simplified — flex gap 2px, 30px buttons, muted color default, brand-yellow on hover. Execution order section: title restyled to 0.72rem uppercase secondary.

JobsTab.vue: message composer restyled — smooth-border panel (bg-raised, border-radius 16px), recipient buttons as pill-shaped with smooth-border inactive / tinted yellow active. Input with bg-elevated, smooth-border, focus ring with yellow 30% opacity. Send button border-radius 8px (square).

AgentTableView.vue: agent column converted from round v-avatar to square tinted badge (32px, border-radius 8px). Added getAgentBadgeStyle() with .hex extraction from composable color object. Agent cell uses flex layout with name 0.8rem + skills 0.62rem muted.

AgentTableView.vue: messages waiting column converted to tinted circular badges (same zero/has-msgs pattern as JobsTab). Table styling: smooth-border panel (border-radius 16px), uppercase 0.6rem headers, muted header color, subtle cell separators.
AgentTableView.vue: agent-id styling updated to IBM Plex Mono, rgba(255,255,255,0.05) background, secondary color text.
ProjectLaunchView.vue: verified — pure layout container with ProjectTabs delegate and edit dialog. No hardcoded colors, no agent-related styling. No changes needed.
npm run lint: 0 errors, 7 pre-existing warnings (unchanged). npm run test: 96 files, 1893 tests pass, 0 failures.
deviations: ProjectLaunchView.vue required no changes — it is a pure layout container that delegates to ProjectTabs for all visible content. The project header styling referenced in the spec lives within ProjectTabs, not ProjectLaunchView.

Execution mode selector styling was not explicitly changed in LaunchTab.vue because the execution mode toggle lives in ProjectTabs.vue (moved there in Handover 0428), not in LaunchTab. The mockup's exec-mode-bar pattern can be applied to ProjectTabs in a future session if needed.

JobsTab play button changed from inset-border ring pattern (box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 2px) to tinted background pattern (rgba 0.12) to match mockup's copy-btn style. The faded state changed from opacity 0.25 to transparent + opacity 0.2.

AgentTableView getAgentBadgeStyle extracts .hex from the composable's color object (which returns {hex, name, badge, description}), unlike LaunchTab/JobsTab which call getAgentColorConfig().hex directly.
sessions.notes_for_next: 0870k: All orchestration views (LaunchTab, JobsTab, AgentTableView) now use the 0870 design system with tinted square badges, smooth-border panels, accessible text colors, and consistent typography. The execution mode selector lives in ProjectTabs.vue (moved in 0428), not LaunchTab — if it needs restyling to match the mockup's exec-mode-pill pattern, that should happen in a ProjectTabs update. The message composer now uses pill-shaped recipient buttons and smooth-border input instead of Vuetify outlined variant. ActionIcons.vue (used in AgentTableView) was verified unchanged in 0870d — it uses actionConfig colors, not agent colors. StatusChip.vue (used in AgentTableView) was also verified unchanged in 0870d — uses statusConfig colors. The hexToRgba helper is now duplicated in 3 files (LaunchTab, JobsTab, AgentTableView) — consider extracting to a shared utility if more components need it.
cascading_impacts: LaunchTab agent cards no longer have colored borders — the orchestrator-card class was removed. Tests should not rely on v-chip or border-color assertions.
JobsTab agent column no longer renders v-avatar — uses plain div.agent-badge with inline tinted styles instead
JobsTab play button no longer uses inset-border ring — now uses tinted background pattern matching mockup
JobsTab message waiting badge now has tinted .msg-badge.zero / .msg-badge.has-msgs classes — tests asserting on .message-count or .message-waiting classes will need updating
AgentTableView agent column no longer renders v-avatar — uses plain div.agent-badge with inline tinted styles instead
AgentTableView messages waiting column now renders .msg-badge instead of plain text — tests asserting on text-warning class will need updating
sessions.summary: Restyled 3 orchestration view files to match 0870 design system. LaunchTab: square tinted agent badges replacing round colored avatars, smooth-border staging panels with uppercase headers, status colors via SCSS tokens. JobsTab: tinted agent badges in table, tinted phase badges, tinted msg-waiting badges (green zero/orange has-msgs), smooth-border table panel and composer, play button with tinted bg, muted action icons. AgentTableView: tinted badges replacing v-avatar, tinted msg badges, updated table/header styling. ProjectLaunchView: no changes needed (pure layout). 3 files changed (405 insertions, 249 deletions), all 1893 tests pass.

tasks_completed: MessagesView.vue: subtitle text-medium-emphasis replaced with scoped messages-text-muted class (#8895a8).

BroadcastPanel.vue: both cards (Send Message, Broadcast History) converted from variant='outlined' to variant='flat' with smooth-border class, bg-raised background, border-radius 16px. All text-medium-emphasis replaced with broadcast-text-muted class (#8895a8).

BroadcastPanel.vue: broadcast history v-avatar replaced with square tinted status-badge (36px, border-radius 8px, rgba(statusColor, 0.15) bg + statusColor text). Status chip converted from v-chip to tinted span. Recipient count chip converted from v-chip to tinted span (blue rgba bg).
BroadcastPanel.vue: added hexToRgba(), getStatusBadgeStyle(), getStatusChipStyle() helpers and STATUS_COLORS map (delivered/completed: #67bd6d, failed: #E07872, pending: #EDBA4A).

MessageItem.vue: v-card converted from variant='outlined' to variant='flat' with smooth-border class, bg-raised background, border-radius 12px. Round v-avatar (40px) replaced with square sender-badge div (36px, border-radius 8px, rgba(senderHex, 0.15) bg + senderHex text). Sender colors mapped to Luminous Pastels: broadcast=#EDBA4A, system=#8895a8, user=#AC80CC (reviewer purple), orchestrator=#D4B08A, default=#6DB3E4 (implementer blue).

MessageItem.vue: priority and status v-chip elements replaced with tinted-chip spans. Priority colors: urgent=#E07872, high=#EDBA4A. Status colors: completed=#67bd6d, failed=#E07872, acknowledged/delivered=#6DB3E4, pending=#EDBA4A. All text-medium-emphasis replaced with msg-text-muted class (#8895a8).

MessageItem.vue: broadcast-message class changed from border-left solid to --smooth-border-color: rgba(237,186,74,0.3) for consistent smooth-border treatment. Hover changed from box-shadow to subtle rgba(255,255,255,0.02) bg.
MessageList.vue: empty state text-grey-lighten-1 replaced with list-text-muted class (#8895a8).

MessagePanel.vue: message history card converted from variant='outlined' to variant='flat' with smooth-border class, bg-raised background, border-radius 16px. Message count v-chip replaced with tinted count-chip span (blue rgba bg). WebSocket connection v-chip replaced with tinted connection-chip span (live: green rgba, off: red rgba). Loading text-medium-emphasis replaced with panel-text-muted class (#8895a8).
npm run lint: 0 errors, 7 pre-existing warnings (unchanged). npm run test: 96 files, 1893 tests pass, 0 failures.
deviations: MessageItem sender colors use Luminous Pastels from the agent palette rather than Vuetify theme colors: orchestrator=#D4B08A, user=#AC80CC (reviewer purple as closest match), broadcast=#EDBA4A (tester yellow), system=#8895a8 (muted grey), default agent=#6DB3E4 (implementer blue).
BroadcastPanel status colors use explicit hex values (#67bd6d, #E07872, #EDBA4A) matching the semantic status palette rather than Vuetify color names, for consistent tinted badge rendering.
sessions.notes_for_next: 0870l: All message view components now use the 0870 design system with tinted square badges, smooth-border panels, accessible text colors, and tinted chips. The hexToRgba helper is now duplicated in 4 files (LaunchTab, JobsTab, AgentTableView, BroadcastPanel) and inline in MessageItem — consider extracting to a shared utility if more components need it in 0870l+. MessageItem no longer uses v-avatar or v-chip — tests asserting on those classes will need updating. The sender badge uses inline styles via computed senderBadgeStyle rather than CSS classes, matching the pattern from 0870j's agent badges. Status colors in BroadcastPanel and MessageItem use the same hex values (#67bd6d for success, #E07872 for error, #EDBA4A for warning) — these are consistent with the semantic status palette but are hardcoded rather than referencing CSS variables.
cascading_impacts: MessageItem no longer renders v-avatar — tests asserting on .v-avatar class will need updating
MessageItem priority and status no longer render as v-chip — tests asserting on .v-chip class will need updating
BroadcastPanel history items no longer render v-avatar or v-chip — tests asserting on those will need updating
MessagePanel message count and connection status no longer render as v-chip — tests asserting on v-chip will need updating
MessageItem broadcast-message class no longer uses border-left — uses --smooth-border-color instead
sessions.summary: Harmonized 5 message view files to 0870 design system. MessagesView: accessible subtitle color. BroadcastPanel: smooth-border cards, square tinted status badges replacing round v-avatar, tinted status/recipient chips. MessageItem: square tinted sender badges (36px, 8px radius) replacing round v-avatar (40px), tinted priority/status chips replacing v-chip, smooth-border card, broadcast highlight via --smooth-border-color. MessageList: accessible empty state text. MessagePanel: smooth-border card, tinted count/connection chips replacing v-chip. 5 files changed (220 insertions, 66 deletions), all 1893 tests pass.

deviations: ProductIntroTour panel cards had a CSS gradient border trick using ::before pseudo-element with mask-composite. The smooth-border class replaces the outlined variant but the gradient border ::before pseudo-element CSS remains in the scoped styles — it still applies over the smooth-border for a branded gradient effect on hover/display.

NetworkSettingsTab CORS Allowed Origins heading was previously text-h6 text-medium-emphasis. Rather than a scoped class, the heading uses an inline style color (#a3aac4 = --text-secondary) since it's a single heading element.

OrganizationSettings Danger Zone card used Vuetify color=error variant=outlined. Replaced with smooth-border + custom --smooth-border-color using rgba(224,120,114,0.3) (analyzer coral at 30% opacity) to maintain the danger visual cue.
sessions.notes_for_next: 0870m: All settings views and supporting components now use the 0870 design system with smooth-border cards, accessible text colors, and consistent styling. The hexToRgba utility is now centralized at frontend/src/utils/colorUtils.js — any future component needing hex-to-rgba conversion should import from there. The integration cards (Git, MCP, Serena) no longer use variant=outlined — they use variant=flat + smooth-border class. Tests that asserted on variant=outlined or text-medium-emphasis in these files have been updated. ContextPriorityConfig depth chips still use Vuetify v-chip with color=grey-darken-1 — these could be converted to tinted spans in a future pass if desired, but they're functional UI elements not decorative badges. ProductIntroTour still has the gradient ::before border effect in CSS — this coexists with the smooth-border class (smooth-border provides the base border, gradient ::before overlays it).
cascading_impacts: hexToRgba is now a shared utility — any new component needing this function should import from @/utils/colorUtils instead of defining locally
Integration cards no longer render variant=outlined — tests asserting on variant=outlined will fail (already updated in this session)
text-medium-emphasis replaced with scoped classes in integration cards — tests asserting on that Vuetify class will fail (already updated)
Settings tab cards now have smooth-border class — any downstream styling targeting .v-card--variant-outlined or .v-card--variant-elevated will not match these cards
sessions.summary: Harmonized 11 settings view files to 0870 design system: 3 main views (UserSettings, SystemSettings, OrganizationSettings), 4 settings tabs (IdentityTab, NetworkSettingsTab, SecuritySettingsTab, SystemPromptTab), 3 integration cards (Git, MCP, Serena), and 2 supporting components (ContextPriorityConfig, ProductIntroTour). All cards converted to variant=flat + smooth-border with bg-raised background. All text-medium-emphasis replaced with scoped accessible classes (#8895a8). Updated 2 test files. 20 files changed (176 insertions, 76 deletions), all 1893 tests pass.
